verdict.answer_first: B: first line gives the bottom line, "swallows every failure silently"; A starts with a label, "classic \"swallow everything\" anti-pattern," then explains.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits harder: "If no, don't catch it at all" and "empty /* ignore */ is... almost always wrong"; A lists three "Better options" plus a scan offer.
verdict.fabrication: A adds an unverifiable external claim, "most linters... will flag this"; B also adds external JS/linter context but less central. No concrete fake metric in either.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to apply because it reduces the prescription to one fork: "recoverable or not"; A has more snippets but more competing paths.
